metadata.name: ai.quickdeploy/paychex
metadata.title: Paychex
metadata.description: Generated Paychex Time Web Services (TWS) SOAP web service MCP catalog manifest.

endpoint.description: SOAP endpoint override for the Paychex TWS ServiceRouter service.

requestTimeoutMs.description: Per-request SOAP upstream timeout in milliseconds.

notes: Official Paychex Time Web Services (TWS) API documentation portal: https://paychex.centralservers.com/service/home/about (also mirrored at https://paychex.flextime.paychex.com/service/Home/About).

TWS is Paychex's private-labeled Time & Attendance / Stratustime SOAP+XML+JSON web service; the SOAP surface is exposed by the ServiceRouter class in the AppOne.Services.V2 namespace, documented at https://paychex.centralservers.com/service/Help/Docs/html/1d583575-d7da-c95a-cd9f-90bd013672d9.htm ("V2 Endpoints").

TWS SOAP endpoints are per-customer-alias (multi-tenant), following the base pattern https://<host>/service/ws-soap/<version>/ServiceRouter.asmx; no fixed public WSDL URL is published, so the committed config.defaults.endpoint models the documented endpoint shape rather than a live tenant URL - same precedent as Workday (QUI-381).

Authentication requires calling CreateToken with CustomerAlias/SharedKey/UserName/UserPass to obtain a security token, which is then passed as the Auth-Token HTTP header on subsequent SOAP calls; modeled here as an api-key auth env ref.
The committed manifest selects read-only GetUserBasic and GetTimeOffPolicy ServiceRouter operations only.

network: SOAP https://paychex.centralservers.com/service/ws-soap/2.0/ServiceRouter.asmx ServiceRouter/GetUserBasic for selected upstream operation
SOAP https://paychex.centralservers.com/service/ws-soap/2.0/ServiceRouter.asmx ServiceRouter/GetTimeOffPolicy for selected upstream operation
process: Run pnpm build/test scripts only inside OpenShell-backed MXC isolation
Run node only as invoked by generated project package scripts inside OpenShell-backed MXC isolation
